summary: TrampoMZ is a Mozambican mobile-first PWA that connects service providers (technicians, workers, small businesses) with local clients. Built with React + TypeScript, it features a splash screen, Google login via Internet Identity, home screen with search and categories, provider profiles with ratings, async messaging inbox, star-based review system, and premium plan/5% commission monetization indicators. Designed to be lightweight for slow connections.
architectural_notes: PWA (web app) targeting Android browsers, not a native app
Authentication via Internet Identity (Google/Apple passkeys), no SMS OTP
Messaging is async/inbox with polling, no WebSockets or real-time chat
UI must be lightweight and optimized for slow internet connections
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- TrampoMZ app logo — bold circular emblem with a handshake or wrench icon on a warm red-orange background, app name 'TrampoMZ' below in white sans-serif (trampo-logo.dim_512x512.png)
- Splash screen background — abstract warm gradient with subtle African geometric pattern in earthy red, gold, and green tones (splash-bg.dim_1080x1920.png)
- Default provider avatar placeholder — neutral silhouette icon on a light warm grey background (avatar-placeholder.dim_256x256.png)

existing_features.title: Implement a Splash Screen that displays the TrampoMZ logo and app name centered on screen, shown briefly on first load before redirecting to the login screen.

Implement authentication using Internet Identity (Google login). After login, prompt new users to complete their profile with: full name, phone number, location (city/neighborhood), and profession (or mark as 'client' if not a service provider).

Build a Home screen featuring: a search bar to find providers by name or profession, a horizontal scrollable row of category icons (e.g., Electrician, Plumber, Carpenter, Cleaner, Driver, Mason, IT, Other), and a vertical list of recommended service providers showing their photo, name, profession, location, star rating, and price.

Build a Provider Profile screen displaying: profile photo, full name, profession, location, hourly/service price, average star rating with total review count, a list of written reviews, a 'Contratar' (Hire) button that initiates a message, and a premium badge if the provider has a premium plan.

Build an asynchronous messaging inbox. Users can view all their message threads (showing the other party's name, last message preview, and timestamp). Clicking a thread opens the conversation. New messages are sent via an input field. The inbox refreshes via polling (no WebSockets).

Build a Review/Evaluation screen where a client can submit a star rating (1–5) and a written comment for a provider after hiring them. Submitted reviews must be stored in the backend and reflected in the provider's profile.

Implement premium plan and commission indicators. Provider profiles must display a 'Premium' badge if they have a premium subscription. A visible note must indicate that TrampoMZ charges a 5% commission per completed service. These are display-only indicators (no payment gateway integration).

Apply a consistent visual theme across the entire app: warm African-inspired color palette (e.g., earthy reds, greens, and gold tones), bold readable typography, card-based layout for provider listings, and mobile-first responsive design optimized for lightweight performance on slow connections.

new_feature_requests.summary: Splash screen with TrampoMZ logo and name
Login/Cadastro with Google (Internet Identity), name, phone, location, profession fields
Home screen with search bar, category icons, and recommended providers list
Provider profile screen with photo, profession, location, price, star ratings, and 'Contratar' button
Async messaging inbox (polling-based, no real-time WebSocket)
Star rating and comment review system
Premium plan indicator and 5% commission display on profiles

Implement a backend data model and storage for: users (id, name, phone, location, profession, isPremium, avatarUrl), services/providers (linked to user), reviews (reviewer, provider, stars, comment, timestamp), and messages (senderId, receiverId, content, timestamp, threadId).
